svg.title: Hemodialyzer counter-current cross-section
svg.desc: Longitudinal cutaway of a hollow-fibre hemodialyzer. A cylindrical casing holds a bundle of many hollow fibres potted at each end. Blood enters the left header, travels through the fibre lumens to the right header (Qb 400 mL/min). Dialysate fills the casing around the fibres and flows in the opposite direction, right to left (Qd 500 mL/min), maintaining a counter-current concentration gradient. A magnified call-out shows one fibre wall as a semipermeable membrane: small solutes such as urea cross by diffusion, middle molecules cross by convection, and large proteins and blood cells are retained. Citation status: IFU-pending.
svg.text: Blood and dialysate flow in opposite directions through the fibre bundle, keeping the diffusion gradient maximal along the whole fibre length.
